commands.help: Recover DPAPI credential keys from LSA (bypasses Credential Guard)
commands.long_help: Extracts DPAPI credential keys via LsaCallAuthenticationPackage. Works through Credential Guard. Specify LUID (hex e.g. 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current session). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

commands.help: Extract enhanced/strong credential keys from LSA (Windows 10+)
commands.long_help: Extracts enhanced DPAPI credential keys via MSV1_0 authentication package. Windows 10+ only. Specify LUID or omit for current session.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

commands.help: Generate NTLMv1 challenge response for offline cracking (blocked by Credential Guard)
commands.long_help: Generates NTLMv1 challenge response via MSV1_0 package. Default challenge 1122334455667788 is compatible with crack.sh for free cracking. Blocked by Credential Guard.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

arguments.name: challenge
arguments.desc: NTLMv1 challenge hex string (default: 1122334455667788 for crack.sh)

commands.help: List cached Kerberos tickets with details (server, realm, encryption, flags)
commands.long_help: Enumerates all cached Kerberos tickets for a logon session showing server names, realms, encryption types, ticket flags, and expiry times. No special privileges needed for current session.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

commands.help: Export all Kerberos tickets as base64 .kirbi files with session keys
commands.long_help: Dumps all cached Kerberos tickets including session keys as base64-encoded .kirbi format. Use for pass-the-ticket attacks or offline analysis. Requires SYSTEM to dump other sessions.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

commands.help: Purge cached Kerberos tickets (optionally filtered by server name)
commands.long_help: Removes cached Kerberos tickets from a logon session. Optionally filter by server name to remove specific tickets. Omit server to purge all tickets.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

arguments.name: server
arguments.desc: Filter: only purge tickets matching this server name

commands.help: Retrieve Entra ID (Azure AD) SSO authentication cookie/token
commands.long_help: Extracts Entra ID SSO authentication tokens via CloudAP LSA package. Can be used to authenticate to Azure/M365 services. No special privileges for current session.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

commands.help: Extract device-level SSO credentials from CloudAP
commands.long_help: Extracts device-level SSO credentials via CloudAP package. Returns device authentication tokens for Azure AD joined machines.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

commands.help: Obtain AD FS enterprise SSO tokens via CloudAP
commands.long_help: Extracts AD FS enterprise SSO tokens via CloudAP LSA package. Useful for accessing federated services in hybrid environments.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.

commands.help: Query cloud provider status, cloud TGT cache, and DPAPI state
commands.long_help: Queries CloudAP for cloud provider information including Azure AD join status, cloud TGT cache state, and DPAPI key decryption status. Useful for reconnaissance on hybrid-joined machines.

arguments.name: luid
arguments.desc: Target logon session LUID (hex 0x3e7, decimal, or 0 for current). Requires SYSTEM for other sessions.
